_notes: Tier system (v3, 2026-05-20): 1 = no district policy; 2 = partial / non-hardware (off-and-away, instructional-time-only, etc.); 3 = hardware-level removal but scope-limited (K-8 only, HS only, has lunch/passing-time access, or has non-medical discretionary exceptions); 4 = bell-to-bell hardware-level K-12 with NO discretionary exceptions (federally-mandated medical/IEP/504 accommodations allowed). Tier 4 currently empty: candidates require live-source verification before promotion. No-mistakes policy: do not elevate to tier 4 from unsourced or AI-generated summary text. Sources arrays may include broader edtech news/action even when the tier itself is set strictly on phone policy.

2502790.policySummary: BPS Code of Conduct prohibits student cell-phone use during the school day district-wide (off-and-away baseline; second offense triggers parent-only return). Yondr-style hardware ban layered on top at ~31 BPS schools (4+ high schools, 9 elementary) under a 3-year contract — opt-in by school, not district-wide.

sources.title: BPS Code of Conduct — Mobile (Cell) Phones
Some high schoolers say a school cell phone ban boosted grades and connections
As State Mulls Cellphone Ban, English High Provides Model Already in Use

2504830.policySummary: Yondr pouch program at Durfee HS, three middle schools (Talbot, Morton, Kuss), and grades 6-8 at Henry Lord and Doran. Effective October 2024; School Committee approved unanimously in August 2024. Elementary grades K-5 not covered.

sources.title: New cell phone policy begins for certain Fall River Public Schools
Cell phone use to be off limits for students at several Fall River Public Schools

2507740.policySummary: District-wide ban on cell-phone use during instructional time, finalized August 2024. NOT Yondr — students place phones in clear bins or pocket charts during class; phones may stay in bags if unseen. Also enforced at school events (e.g. dances). IEP/504 carve-outs.

sources.title: Methuen schools ban cellphones, superintendent says devices are "addictions" for students
Methuen schools finalize student cell phone ban in classrooms

2508610.policySummary: District-wide "Off & Away" policy adopted unanimously by School Committee Sept 2024. Yondr pouches in elementary and middle schools all day; high schools (Newton North, Newton South) require phones stored away during academic time only — no Yondr at HS level. Day Middle and Newton North piloted 2023-24.

sources.title: Do Not Disturb: Newton starts school year with new cell phone policy
Cell phones at school: Parents sound alarm, NPS discusses ways to limit access

2503150.policySummary: Public Schools of Brookline: bell-to-bell phone-free policy for K-8. Phones must be put away at start of day and not used until dismissal. (User-verified 2026-05-20; supersedes earlier handbook auto-extraction that mis-pulled a same-named district in NH.)

sources.title: School districts across the country are rolling out new policies for mobile devices. What's Brookline's plan?
Brookline School Committee Considers New Mobile Device Policy

2512660.policySummary: Phone-free at all elementary schools and at both middle schools (Blanchard, Stony Brook), where Yondr pouches have been in use since 2024. Westford Academy (HS) operates on an off-and-away basis: no use during class without teacher permission, allowed in cafeteria and between classes.

sources.title: Westford educators react to proposed school cell phone ban

2508580.policySummary: Newburyport: phones off when school starts, stored in bags; partial bell-to-bell restriction in classrooms and hallways.

2506480.policySummary: Ipswich: students lock phones in caddies at start of day; true bell-to-bell — no use at lunch, passing time, or any other time.

2503090.policySummary: District-wide Yondr pouch requirement for grades 7-12. Pouches distributed each morning during Breakfast & Advisory, locked in front of staff, kept by student all day. School Committee approved unanimously in March 2024 amid safety/violence concerns at Brockton HS. K-6 not covered.

sources.title: Stricter cellphone policy for Brockton High School approved amid reports of violence
Details of new Brockton High School secure cell phone policy revealed
How locking up phones has changed life inside Brockton High School

2507020.policySummary: Lowell HS implemented hardware-based phone collection (Nightlock units installed summer 2023; ~1,200 storage units). Phones placed in classroom containers at start of class; allowed in hallways and at lunch. HS-only policy — middle and elementary scope unverified.

sources.title: New cell phone policy at Lowell High School prompts pushback from students
Lowell High wants to lock up cell phones. Students are fighting back.

2508430.policySummary: Roosevelt Middle uses Yondr pouches (~800 students, $16K spend); Normandin Middle uses homeroom lockboxes. New Bedford HS originally planned full Yondr but reduced to violator-only Yondr after student pushback. District announced 'Disconnect to Reconnect' Yondr expansion to grades 6-12 starting 2025-26.

sources.title: New cell phone policies for Normandin, Roosevelt, and New Bedford High
New Bedford Schools outline plan to roll out Yondr pouches after middle-school cellphone enforcement showed early gains

2510470.policySummary: Cited by Boston Globe (Aug 2024) as among MA districts implementing 2024-25 cell-phone restrictions. Specific mechanism and grade scope not yet verified against district handbook.

sources.title: Cellphone bans in schools: Mass. school districts crack down

2503660.policySummary: Chicopee High School uses Yondr pouches since 2022; phones locked + staff receives locked pouch upon entry.

2511130.policySummary: Springfield Central HS deploys Yondr pouches since 2022. District-wide policy across the ~50 schools in Springfield not verified — likely school-by-school.

sources.title: Cell phones have been taking over classrooms. These Boston-area schools are making a stand.

2511400.policySummary: Sutton HS named among Massachusetts schools implementing phone restrictions; specific mechanism not verified against district handbook.

sources.title: Cell phones have been taking over classrooms. These Boston-area schools are making a stand.

2503270.policySummary: All middle and high schools (grades 6-12 incl. Cambridge Rindge and Latin School) collect phones at the start of each period using cubbies / clear pencil cases / cardboard boxes; phones returned at end of each period. Phones allowed at lunch, between classes, before/after school. Effective fall 2024 — no formal Yondr deployment yet.

sources.title: Cambridge Schools Go Analog With No-Phones Policy
As Mass. Legislators Work to Ban Phones in Schools, CPS Students See Ups and Downs

2506840.policySummary: School Committee approved February 2025: pre-K through grade 8 banned during school day (no devices visible); grades 9-12 must place phones in teacher-designated holding area during class. Honor-system / off-and-away — no Yondr. Implementation rollout was uneven; not all parents received district communication after approval.

sources.title: New cellphone policy in Lexington Public Schools

2512270.policySummary: K-8 ban on phones, smart watches and personal devices; Wellesley Middle School uses Yondr pouches (entry-to-exit) funded by Wellesley Education Foundation. Wellesley HS allows teacher-permission use; in academic spaces phones go on silent in the classroom's designated space for the class period. Effective 2025-26 SY.

sources.title: New Cell Phone and Smart Device Procedures for Students
Cell phones in the Wellesley schools — an issue that cries out for stronger policies

2513230.policySummary: School Committee approved revised policy in 2024: cell phone use barred during instructional time without teacher permission for educational purposes; limited use allowed during lunch and other non-instructional time for educational communication (email, chat). Off-and-away enforcement.

sources.title: Massachusetts eyes limits on student phone, social media use

2509870.policySummary: Quincy School Committee subcommittee unanimously moved a strict all-day Yondr pouch policy out of committee in April 2026; full School Committee vote pending. Coverage: every middle and high school student. Phones turned off and locked in Yondr at start of day, kept by student, unlocked at dismissal.

sources.title: Mass. House to vote on youth social media ban, strict school cellphone ban

2511520.policySummary: Taunton HS uses Yondr pouches: phones turned off and locked at start of school day, carried by student all day, unlocked at dismissal. Cited by Quincy School Committee as a peer with reported gains (improved grades; reduced bullying, suspensions, fighting, cheating). Middle/elementary scope not verified.

sources.title: Taunton High School announces cell phone ban
Stop the Yondr Pouch Implementation in Taunton High School! (petition)

2508370.policySummary: Needham HS uses 'phone hotels' (shoe-bag-style hangers near classroom doors) — students place phones in assigned slots for class duration. K-8: phones discouraged; if brought, stored in coat/backpack/cubby. School Committee approved late May 2024. Explicitly NOT Yondr.

sources.title: New approaches cut cell phone use in schools
Community Weighs Value of Phones in Schools

2504980.policySummary: Framingham HS adopted no-cell-phones-in-classroom restriction; allowed in hallways and at lunch. District-wide K-12 scope not yet verified. (Christa McAuliffe Charter School in Framingham uses Yondr but is a charter, not district.)

sources.title: Educators Seek Restrictions on Phones, Electronic Devices

2503870.policySummary: Concord-Carlisle HS handbook: students store phones in caddies during class time; access between classes and at lunch. Regional School Committee opted to hold off on stricter rules in 2025; 'Cell Phone Policy Process' on December 2025 committee agenda — district still actively evaluating.

sources.title: Hold the phone: School boards weigh cell bans during class time
Concord schools need a phone-free policy

2512210.policySummary: Wayland HS administrative council enacted policy October 2024: phones kept in secure holders or powered off in backpacks; in-class enforcement at teacher discretion. Policy followed extensive community input (~800 parent + ~800 student + ~200 staff survey responses); >80% MS/HS parents supported clear expectations.

sources.title: Parents Favor Blanket Cell Phone Bans, Schools Lean Towards Nuanced Approach

2501710.policySummary: Acton-Boxborough Regional HS for 2025-26 SY: cell phones placed in classroom cubbies during class; allowed during free periods and lunch in cafe / student centers / non-class times. NOT Yondr. Elementary/middle scope not yet verified.

sources.title: Acton-Boxborough Regional School Committee policy meeting (Apr 17 2025)

2509720.policySummary: Plymouth Public Schools: 'put away for the day' approach in force ~2 years (since ~2023). Phones in students' bags or teacher classroom cases; only used when instructor requests for educational purposes. Specific grade-level scope and mechanism details not fully verified.

sources.title: Plymouth Schools Ahead Of Curve Banning Cell Phones

2502490.policySummary: Belmont HS adopted policy requiring phones off-and-away in all classes from fall 2024; phones stored in cell phone holders during class. Replaces prior teacher-discretion regime. Approved by School Committee in June 2024.

sources.title: Belmont High to Ban Cell Phones in the Classroom
Belmont High Considering In-Class Cell Phone Ban

2507260.policySummary: Marblehead HS: students may not use phones during class hours; use allowed between classes and at lunch. Collection is at individual teacher's discretion (principal explicitly framed as preparing students for 'real world responsibility'). Off-and-away — not Yondr.

sources.title: MHS Implements New Security, Cell Phone, Attendance Policies

2509990.policySummary: Middle school: phones turned off and stored in lockers during the school day. Reading HS handbook contains no cell phone policy. Statewide mandate (S.2561, fall 2026) will force a HS policy.

sources.title: School cell phone policies still developing as academic year begins

2508340.policySummary: Natick HS Student Handbook (2023-24, in force): phones must be off and not visible in classrooms during class periods (also in bathrooms during the school day). Use allowed in dining hall, courtyard, and other designated areas. Teachers may approve educational use in class. Off-and-away enforcement.

2506090.policySummary: Hingham HS handbook: cell phones, smart watches, headphones must be silenced and put away when entering classroom; teacher may permit use for academic purposes. Allowed in cafeteria at lunch and at non-instructional times. Faculty/admin may confiscate non-compliant devices.

sources.title: HHS Student Handbook & Code of Discipline 2025-2026

2512180.policySummary: Watertown HS piloting the Doorman app in 2025-26 SY (first public school in the country) — software 'bricks' / disables cellphone communication during class periods. Separately, a district-wide draft cell-phone policy is in development per the School Committee.

sources.title: Tap into class: Watertown High will be first in country to test new cell phone-limiting tech
Watertown High School to Pilot App That Would Disable Student Cellphones During Class

2504410.policySummary: Duxbury uses classroom caddy/hotel approach; phones placed in designated storage upon entering classroom.

2507560.policySummary: Handbook reviewed: rted to law enforcement officials. E. Hazing See Violation of Chapter 536 - Hazing in this handbook. F. Pattern of Behavior The repetition or combination of disciplinary infractions justifying Tier I

sources.title: What students, teachers and parents think about banning phones in Mass. schools

2508850.policySummary: Board-approved June 12, 2025 (Cell Phone Policy Direction A): all cell phones and electronic devices collected at the beginning of the day by staff and returned at end of day. Students must power down and secure devices in school-provided pouches upon entry. Earbuds/headphones banned without explicit teacher permission. Earned/lost as a privilege. District-wide K-12.

tier_history.reason: Bell-to-bell K-12 hardware-level, no broad exceptions
Demoted: needs live-source verification before claiming tier-4. Either had explicit non-medical exception language, scope marked unknown, or zero sources on file.

2501920.policySummary: Amherst Regional HS current policy: phones in cell phone pouches when entering classroom; access between classes and at lunch. New 'bell-to-bell' policy approved for September 2026 implementation per Amherst Indy reporting. Currently tier 2 (hardware in classroom only).

sources.title: New Bell to Bell Cell Phone Policy on Deck for September 2026 Brings Mixed Feelings at ARHS

2502310.policySummary: Barnstable HS bans cell phone use; district-wide cell phone policy under review. Cape News reporting from 2024 confirms HS-level prohibition; mechanism (off-and-away vs. hardware) and elementary scope not yet verified.

sources.title: Mashpee School Committee Debates Cellphone Policy (references Barnstable)

2507440.policySummary: Mashpee schools' cell phone policy restricts use during school hours but allows use during homeroom, breakfast, and lunch. School Committee debated in August 2024 whether to tighten the policy to match neighboring Sandwich.

sources.title: Mashpee School Committee Debates Cellphone Policy

2509360.policySummary: Peabody School Committee approved restrictions on student cell phone use. Peabody Veterans HS implemented a pilot policy that bans cell phones during academic periods, with reasonable exceptions for medical conditions, IEP, off-campus travel, and emergencies. Tier-2 off-and-away during instructional time.

sources.title: Peabody Veterans High To Ban Cell Phones In Class Starting This Fall

2509630.policySummary: Revised Pittsfield Public Schools cell phone policy in force; on a second infraction students' phones are held in the main office. Reported as effective: 156 referrals (significant decrease from prior years) and described by educators as a 'definite deterrent' due to clear policy and well-defined consequences.

sources.title: Pittsfield Schools' New Cell Phone Policy Effective So Far

2506750.policySummary: Lenox bans cellphone use by middle school students. Some Lenox schools allow no usage during classes without teacher permission; access during nonclass time. Tier 2 (partial — focused at middle school).

sources.title: Lenox bans cellphone use by middle school students

2505740.policySummary: Hampshire Regional HS requires students to store phones in pouches during the school day. Hardware-style enforcement at the high-school level.

sources.title: Local schools weigh in on proposed statewide cellphone ban

2505490.policySummary: Greenfield Public School District uses Yondr pouches: students place phones in small gray pouches sealed magnetically at the beginning of the day; pouches can only be opened at the end of the day at a magnet station. Bell-to-bell hardware enforcement.

sources.title: Local schools weigh in on proposed statewide cellphone ban

2504590.policySummary: Easthampton Public Schools allow cellphone use before/after school, between classes, and at lunch. During class times students store phones in a phone pouch maintained by the teacher unless specifically allowed. Off-and-away with light hardware (teacher-held pouches).

sources.title: Local schools weigh in on proposed statewide cellphone ban

2503240.policySummary: Burlington HS effective 2024-25: phones and headphones not allowed during school day except at lunch; devices stored in lockers, backpacks or pockets. Middle schools (Hunt, Edmunds) do not allow phones at all during the school day. Reportedly well-received by teachers and parents.

2512510.policySummary: West Springfield HS implemented phone restrictions in 2024-25: students place phones in pouches when entering, released from pouches when leaving. Superintendent reports improved student engagement and mental-health benefits.

sources.title: Massachusetts lawmakers look to ban cell phone use in schools

2505970.policySummary: School Committee approved district-wide policy July 2024, in force 2024-25. Phones placed in designated classroom container or silent in backpack during class; allowed between classes and at lunch. Confiscation + ban for violations; one-day suspension for unauthorized photo/video.

sources.title: Haverhill Schools Adopt New Policy Limiting Student Phone Use
Haverhill High Principal Downs to Brief School Committee on New Policy Limiting Phone Use

2504860.policySummary: Falmouth HS: cell phones not allowed during class times; silenced and placed in classroom holders. School Board approved April 2026 a stricter policy that bars personal electronic devices during the school day district-wide — full ban applies starting 2026-27 SY.

sources.title: Falmouth schools pass cell phone ban in advance of new state law

2512750.policySummary: School Committee adopted phased policy 2025-04-28. 2025-26 SY: pre-K through grade 8 prohibited from using personal electronic devices during school day; HS may use cellphones during class transitions, lunch, free blocks. 2026-27 SY: full district ban — HS laptops only, placed in designated storage during the day.

sources.title: Weston Public Schools implementing new cellphone policy
Weston School Committee Meeting Minutes Sept 9 2024

2507320.policySummary: Marlborough HS uses 'cell phone hotels' — numbered cubby holes in classrooms where students place phones at the start of class and retrieve at the end. Off-and-away with light hardware enforcement.

2507530.policySummary: Medfield HS Student Handbook (2025-2026): students may not make/take phone calls during the school day. Use of unapproved electronic devices restricted during the school day for all personal/non-academic purposes. Off-and-away enforcement; mechanism details (per-classroom storage etc.) not yet captured.

2512840.policySummary: Weymouth HS implemented cell-phone-free classroom policy: classrooms and common spaces have cell phone holders. Students place phones in holders upon entry and retrieve at end of period. Use allowed during passing, study periods, and lunch.

2506330.policySummary: Hopkinton: phones stored in classroom caddy for entire period; off-campus use allowed before/after school, lunch, study halls.

2502400.policySummary: Bedford HS pilot 'bell-to-bell no cell' policy in 2024-25: phones collected before each class session; access during lunch and other breaks. Cited successful peer districts as model. Hardware-light enforcement (per-class collection).

sources.title: Under Pilot Policy, BHS Students will Forego Phones in Class

2511580.policySummary: Wynn Middle School piloted magnetic pouches (Yondr-style) starting April 2024. Fully implemented for 2024-25 SY at the middle school. Teachers reported up to 60% reduction in student time out of classroom since restriction. Tier-2 — MS hardware in force; HS scope unverified.

sources.title: School cell phone policies still developing as academic year begins

2502670.policySummary: Billerica policy in force 2025-26 SY: all student phones and smartwatches kept in student lockers, turned off, for the duration of the school day. Locker access at scheduled times (morning, after period 3, before PE, before last period special, end of day). Hardware-style separation via locker storage.

2510680.policySummary: Shrewsbury HS implemented policy: students place cell phones in 'phone holder' at front of classroom at beginning of each period. Initial student resistance but described as a positive change to classroom environment. Off-and-away enforcement.

sources.title: The Rise of Phone Bans in Schools (Shrewsbury reference)

2507110.policySummary: Updated district-wide policy effective Sept 1, 2025: cell phones must be off and not visible during the school day, including during lunch and in hallways. Progressive discipline for repeat violations (parent contact → confiscation → detention → suspension). IEP/504/health-plan accommodations.

sources.title: Lynn Public Schools ring out new cell-phone rules

2503990.policySummary: Danvers HS current policy allows phones during lunch and passing periods; prohibits use during instruction unless teacher permits. Reported inconsistent enforcement across classrooms. School Committee discussed an update June 2025 with new principal Alan Strauss.

sources.title: DHS discusses new cell phone policy amid state legislative changes

2511220.policySummary: Stoneham HS handbook: cellular telephones and headphones/earbuds put away before entering all classrooms. Use allowed during lunch and hall passing.

sources.title: School cell phone policies still developing as academic year begins

2507860.policySummary: Milford HS implemented a policy prohibiting cell-phone use in classrooms and hallways since 2019 — one of the earlier MA districts to adopt restrictions. Off-and-away enforcement.

sources.title: Mass. education department 'likely' to encourage schools to restrict phones (Milford reference)

2509600.policySummary: Pioneer Valley Regional: full blanket ban on personal electronic devices during school day; 95% reduction in cafeteria phone use post-implementation.

2510380.policySummary: Salem High School uses Yondr pouches (since 2023-24). Two-strike enforcement: warning, then phone to front office, then parent involvement. Middle and elementary scope not verified — likely tier 1 elsewhere in district.

sources.title: Why some Mass. schools made the call to lock up student phones
Cell phones have been taking over classrooms. These Boston-area schools are making a stand.
